_comment: Turnkey policy attached to the bot's signing key. This file is the
source of truth — changes are PR-reviewed and applied to Turnkey via
`docs/turnkey-setup.md`.
Turnkey's policy schema continues to evolve (selector / `from` set
matching in particular). The fields below reflect the model we
design against in code; the actual JSON you upload may need shape
adjustments. Read this as 'the rules we intend to enforce', not
'the literal Turnkey JSON'.
Only allow txs whose `to` is a Toban-registered ThanksToken
contract. The registry is maintained out of band (env var
`THANKS_TOKEN_ADDRESS` for MVP single-workspace; future: a
subgraph-synced JSON list of addresses uploaded as a Turnkey
tag set).
Function selector for the ThanksToken mint primitive (#506):
mintFrom(address,address,uint256,(uint256,address)[],bytes)
If the contract's `mintFrom` signature ever changes (including
the `RelatedRole` struct shape), recompute and update both this
selector and the matching ABI in `src/chain.ts`.
The decoded `from` argument of mintFrom (the token holder
whose allowance is being spent) must be a wallet known to the
subgraph as IdentityBound. We maintain this as a Turnkey tag
set updated by a periodic worker cron that reads the
subgraph's IdentityBinding entity. TODO: implement the cron
in a follow-up issue.
Defence in depth: the `msg.sender` of mintFrom (set by
Turnkey to the signing key's own address) is automatically
this key, but we re-state it here so a future change that
splits signing across multiple keys doesn't bypass it.
mintFrom is non-payable; reject any tx that attaches ETH to
avoid funding bugs from draining the gas balance.
Example rate limit; Turnkey's rate-limit primitive may be
named differently or live outside policy. Adjust when
applying. The intent: cap mintFrom rate per signer key per
minute to limit blast radius of a stamper-key compromise.

_notes: 'toban-identity-bound-wallets') are intended to be kept in sync by
an out-of-band worker / cron that reads the Toban subgraph and
calls Turnkey's tag-update API. Until that cron exists, we operate
with a single ThanksToken address and an explicit allowlist.
The selector `0x40062e89` is bound to the canonical signature
see `src/chain.ts` for the matching ABI fragment.
